# terrible_community_general_pkgutil (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against
- `name` (String) The name of the package. When using `state=latest`, this can be `'*'`, which updates all installed packages managed by pkgutil.
- `state` (String) Whether to install (`present`/`installed`), or remove (`absent`/`removed`) packages. The upgrade (`latest`) operation updates/installs the packages to the latest version available.

### Optional

- `force` (Boolean) To allow the update process to downgrade packages to match what is present in the repository, set this to `true`. This is useful for rolling back to stable from testing, or similar operations.
- `site` (String) The repository path to install the package from. Its global definition is in `/etc/opt/csw/pkgutil.conf`.
- `update_catalog` (Boolean) If you always want to refresh your catalog from the mirror, even when it is not stale, set this to `true`.
